title: CLAUDE.md
text: autoedit R package providing real-time collaborative code editor widget built CodeMirror 6 Automerge CRDT. connects via WebSocket automerge-repo compatible sync servers multi-user editing automatic conflict resolution.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R: htmlwidgets (required), automerge/autosync/shiny (suggested) JS: @automerge/automerge-repo, @automerge/automerge-codemirror, codemirror

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Automerge document must “text” field type Automerge text. Shiny, editor content available input$<outputId>_content.

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: run example, need autoedit package installed: run app . Open multiple browser windows tabs pointing Shiny app URL see real-time collaboration action.

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
code:
library(shiny) library(autoedit)  # Available meeting rooms (names = display labels, values = IDs) rooms <- c(  "Daily Standup" = "standup",  "Sprint Planning" = "planning",  "Retrospective" = "retrospective",  "Brainstorming" = "brainstorm" )  room_labels <- setNames(names(rooms), rooms)  ui <- fluidPage(  tags$head(    tags$style(HTML("      .notes-container {        border: 1px solid #ddd;        border-radius: 4px;        padding: 0;      }      .notes-container textarea {        font-family: 'SF Mono', Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', Menlo, monospace;        font-size: 14px;        line-height: 1.5;        border: none;        resize: none;      }      .room-header {        background: #f8f9fa;        padding: 10px 15px;        border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd;        border-radius: 4px 4px 0 0;      }      .sync-indicator {        display: inline-block;        width: 8px;        height: 8px;        border-radius: 50%;        background: #28a745;        margin-right: 8px;      }    "))  ),   titlePanel("Collaborative Meeting Notes"),   fluidRow(    column(      width = 3,      wellPanel(        h4("Select Room"),        radioButtons(          "room",          label = NULL,          choices = rooms,          selected = "standup"        ),        hr(),        p(          class = "text-muted",          tags$span(class = "sync-indicator"),          "Sync active"        ),        p(          class = "text-muted small",          "Open multiple browser windows to collaborate in real-time."        ),        hr(),        downloadButton("export", "Export as Markdown", class = "btn-sm")      )     ),     column(      width = 9,      div(        class = "notes-container",        div(          class = "room-header",          h5(            style = "margin: 0;",            textOutput("room_title", inline = TRUE)          )        ),        # Create a textarea for each room (only active one is visible)        div(          id = "standup-panel",          textarea_ui(            "notes_standup",            label = NULL,            width = "100%",            height = "500px",            placeholder = "Start typing your standup notes..."          )        ),        div(          id = "planning-panel",          style = "display: none;",          textarea_ui(            "notes_planning",            label = NULL,            width = "100%",            height = "500px",            placeholder = "Start typing your planning notes..."          )        ),        div(          id = "retrospective-panel",          style = "display: none;",          textarea_ui(            "notes_retrospective",            label = NULL,            width = "100%",            height = "500px",            placeholder = "Start typing your retrospective notes..."          )        ),        div(          id = "brainstorm-panel",          style = "display: none;",          textarea_ui(            "notes_brainstorm",            label = NULL,            width = "100%",            height = "500px",            placeholder = "Start typing your ideas..."          )        )      )    )  ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {  # Initialize textarea servers for each room  # Each room has its own doc_id for independent collaboration  text_standup <- textarea_server(    "notes_standup",    doc_id = "standup",    initial_text = "# Daily Standup

## What I did yesterday
- 

## What I'm doing today
- 

## Blockers
- 
"  )   text_planning <- textarea_server(    "notes_planning",    doc_id = "planning",    initial_text = "# Sprint Planning

## Goals
- 

## User Stories
- 

## Capacity
- 
"  )   text_retrospective <- textarea_server(    "notes_retrospective",    doc_id = "retrospective",    initial_text = "# Retrospective

## What went well
- 

## What could be improved
- 

## Action items
- 
"  )   text_brainstorm <- textarea_server(    "notes_brainstorm",    doc_id = "brainstorm",    initial_text = "# Brainstorming Session

## Ideas
- 

## Discussion Notes
- 
"  )   # Toggle panel visibility based on selected room  observeEvent(input$room, {    all_rooms <- c("standup", "planning", "retrospective", "brainstorm")    for (room in all_rooms) {      panel_id <- paste0(room, "-panel")      if (room == input$room) {        shinyjs_show <- sprintf(          "$('#%s').show();",          panel_id        )      } else {        shinyjs_show <- sprintf(          "$('#%s').hide();",          panel_id        )      }      session$sendCustomMessage("toggle_panel", list(        id = panel_id,        show = room == input$room      ))    }  })   # Add custom message handler for panel toggling  session$onFlushed(function() {    session$sendCustomMessage(      type = "register_toggle",      message = list()    )  }, once = TRUE)   # Room title  output$room_title <- renderText({    room_labels[input$room]  })   # Get current room's text for export  current_text <- reactive({    switch(      input$room,      "standup" = text_standup(),      "planning" = text_planning(),      "retrospective" = text_retrospective(),      "brainstorm" = text_brainstorm(),      ""    )  })   # Export handler  output$export <- downloadHandler(    filename = function() {      paste0(input$room, "-notes-", Sys.Date(), ".md")    },    content = function(file) {      writeLines(current_text(), file)    }  ) }  # UI must include the JavaScript for panel toggling ui <- tagList(  tags$head(    tags$script(HTML("      Shiny.addCustomMessageHandler('toggle_panel', function(msg) {        if (msg.show) {          $('#' + msg.id).show();        } else {          $('#' + msg.id).hide();        }      });    "))  ),  ui )  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: textarea_server() call takes doc_id parameter identifies collaborative document. sessions using doc_id synchronize together:
code: # These will sync together (same doc_id) textarea_server("editor1", doc_id = "shared-doc") textarea_server("editor2", doc_id = "shared-doc")  # This is independent (different doc_id) textarea_server("editor3", doc_id = "private-doc")

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: initial_text parameter sets starting content new document. document already exists (another session created ), parameter ignored:

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: avoid excessive synchronization, text changes debounced. default 150ms, can adjust :
code: # Faster sync (more responsive, more overhead) textarea_server("fast", doc_id = "doc", debounce_ms = 50)  # Slower sync (less responsive, less overhead) textarea_server("slow", doc_id = "doc", debounce_ms = 500)

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: serverless textarea module limitations compared WebSocket-based editor: Single process : Synchronization works within single R process. run multiple Shiny processes (e.g., behind load balancer), sessions different processes won’t sync. persistence: Documents stored memory lost R process restarts. persistent collaboration, use editor() widget external sync server. syntax highlighting: textarea plain text input. code editing syntax highlighting, use editor(). production deployments requiring persistence multi-process scaling, see CodeMirror editor vignette.

text: Real-time collaborative text editing R Shiny applications, built Automerge CRDT.

title: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge
Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge
text: autoedit provides two approaches collaborative editing:

title: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge
text: Shiny module syncs across browser sessions using Shiny’s reactive system. external server required - Shiny process manages document state directly. Open multiple browser windows real-time collaboration.
code: library(shiny) library(autoedit)  ui <- fluidPage(   textarea_ui("editor", label = "Collaborative notes") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   text <- textarea_server("editor", initial_text = "Start typing...") }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge
text: CodeMirror 6 code editor widget syncs via WebSocket automerge-repo compatible server. Suitable collaboration across different applications persistent documents.
code: library(autoedit)  # Connect to a sync server editor("ws://localhost:3030", "document-id")  # Or use the public Automerge sync server editor("wss://sync.automerge.org", "your-document-id")

title: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge
code: library(shiny) library(automerge) library(autosync) library(autoedit)  server <- amsync_server(port = 3030, host = "127.0.0.1") server$start()  doc_id <- create_document(server) doc <- get_document(server, doc_id) am_put(doc, AM_ROOT, "text", am_text("")) am_commit(doc, "init")  ui <- fluidPage(editor_output("editor"))  shiny_server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$editor <- editor_render(editor(server$url, doc_id)) }  onStop(function() server$stop()) shinyApp(ui, shiny_server)

what: autoedit: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge — autoedit-package
title: autoedit: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge — autoedit-package
text: Real-time collaborative text editing R Shiny applications, built 'Automerge' 'CRDT'. Provides 'CodeMirror' 6 editor widget WebSocket sync serverless Shiny module multi-user editing automatic conflict resolution.

title: autoedit: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge — autoedit-package
text: Maintainer: Charlie Gao charlie.gao@posit.co (ORCID) contributors: Posit Software, PBC (ROR) [copyright holder, funder]

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
text: Output render functions using editor within Shiny applications interactive R Markdown documents.

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
code: editor_output(outputId, width = "100%", height = "400px")  editor_render(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
text: outputId Output variable read . width, height Widget dimensions (must valid CSS unit number coerced string "px" appended). expr expression generates editor widget. env environment evaluate expr. quoted Logical, whether expr quoted expression.

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
text: editor_output() returns Shiny output element. editor_render() returns Shiny render function.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: Creates real-time collaborative text editor using CodeMirror 6 Automerge CRDT, synchronized via WebSocket automerge-repo compatible sync server.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: server_url WebSocket URL (ws:// wss://) sync server. doc_id Document ID (base58check encoded). timeout Connection timeout milliseconds. Default 10000. width, height Widget dimensions.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: htmlwidget object.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: widget connects specified sync server synchronizes CodeMirror 6 editor Automerge document. Multiple users can edit document simultaneously automatic conflict resolution. Automerge document must "text" field type Automerge text (created automerge::am_text()). Shiny applications, current editor content available input value input$<outputId>_content.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Connect to the public Automerge sync server editor("wss://sync.automerge.org", "your-document-id")  # Or use with an autosync server server <- autosync::amsync_server(port = 3030) server$start()  doc_id <- autosync::create_document(server) doc <- autosync::get_document(server, doc_id) automerge::am_put(doc, automerge::AM_ROOT, "text", automerge::am_text("")) automerge::am_commit(doc, "init")  editor(server$url, doc_id) } # }

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: Server logic collaborative textarea synchronizes across Shiny sessions using Automerge CRDT.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: id Module ID (must match ID used textarea_ui()). doc_id Document identifier. textareas doc_id synchronize together. Default "default". initial_text Initial text content. used creating new document; ignored document already exists. debounce_ms Debounce delay milliseconds text input changes. Default 150ms.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: reactive expression returning current synchronized text.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: module uses Shiny's reactive system synchronize text across multiple browser sessions without requiring external sync server. session maintains local Automerge document syncs shared master document using Automerge's sync protocol. Concurrent edits automatically merged using Automerge's CRDT algorithm, ensuring eventual consistency across sessions.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(textarea_ui("editor"))   server <- function(input, output, session) textarea_server("editor")   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
text: Creates textarea input synchronizes across Shiny sessions using Automerge CRDT, without requiring external sync server.

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
code: textarea_ui(   id,   label = NULL,   width = "100%",   height = "200px",   placeholder = NULL )

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
text: id Module ID. label Label textarea, NULL label. width width input (e.g., "100%", "400px"). height height input (e.g., "200px"). placeholder Placeholder text empty.

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
text: Shiny UI element.

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(textarea_ui("editor"))   server <- function(input, output, session) textarea_server("editor")   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }
